title: Fix a Broken GitHub Actions Workflow

problem.statement: This GitHub Actions workflow is supposed to run pytest on every push, but the YAML is invalid and the workflow never runs. Identify the missing syntax: what needs to be added to the 'on' trigger line, and what needs to be added to the 'with' key?
problem.code_context:
name: CI
on push:
  branches: [ main ]
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with
          python-version: '3.11'
      - run: pip install -r requirements.txt
      - run: pytest
problem.use_case: YAML is whitespace- and colon-sensitive, and a single missing colon silently breaks an entire CI pipeline with a cryptic parser error — this is one of the most common first bugs anyone hits writing GitHub Actions workflows.

hints: YAML is indentation- and colon-sensitive — check every top-level and nested key for a missing ':'.
The 'on' trigger needs its own colon, with 'push:' nested underneath it, not on the same line.
Every mapping key in YAML (like 'with') needs a trailing colon even if nothing follows on that line — the fix is 'on:' (with 'push:' nested below) and 'with:'.

solution.explanation: Two YAML syntax bugs: 'on push:' needed to become 'on:' with 'push:' nested beneath it, and 'with' needed a trailing colon ('with:') to be a valid mapping key.
